purpose: Phase 1 baseline for Phase 2 regression testing
export_path: Silver (CH forge.observations FINAL) → domain mapping → PyArrow table → per-partition Iceberg merge → MinIO overwrite

dagster_overhead_seconds.description: Run lifecycle (enqueue → subprocess launch → resource init → post-step)

steady_state.description: Hourly incremental export after sync catchup complete

bulk_catchup.description: Catching up after Tiingo deep backfill (20k+ obs flowing through sync)
bulk_catchup.notes: Compute scales linearly with partition count. 99 partitions = historical data spanning many year_month × domain combinations. Steady-state touches only 3 partitions (current month × active domains).

cold_start.description: First-ever export (no prior watermark, all historical data)

regression_thresholds.description: Phase 2 should flag regression if these are exceeded
regression_thresholds.notes: Current steady-state is 3s compute / 14.5s total. Thresholds set at ~3x headroom. If exceeded, investigate PyIceberg merge or MinIO write degradation.

related_benchmarks.notes: FINAL query performance (sub-second at 500k) is not the bottleneck. Export time is dominated by per-partition Iceberg read-merge-overwrite cycle.

key_observations: Dagster overhead is ~10s regardless of workload (subprocess lifecycle cost)
Compute time scales linearly with partition count, not row count
Steady-state (3 partitions) is well under 10s compute
Bulk catchup (99 partitions) takes 2-4 minutes — acceptable for backfill scenarios
No anomaly guard triggers observed in production runs
